title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · History Brief: Pull Request Scope
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git pull request scope increment in a team contribution and review protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Pull Request Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Pull Request Scope contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.

content.text: Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Pull Request Scope condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Git Reference — Controls porcelain and plumbing command behavior, configuration, revision syntax, hooks, workflows, and recovery.

hints: Name the Pull Request Scope precondition or invariant that the theory case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A pull request should include every nearby cleanup to reduce future work.

options.text: A pull request should include every nearby cleanup to reduce future work.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Pull Request Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Pull Request Scope application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.

content.text: Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. A defensible solution applies Pull Request Scope, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a team contribution and review protocol.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 4
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A pull request should include every nearby cleanup to reduce future work. This fails the Pull Request Scope contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Git Reference (current as of 2026-07) — Controls porcelain and plumbing command behavior, configuration, revision syntax, hooks, workflows, and recovery.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Build Pull Request Scope evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-pull-request-scope-yvbgtq" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Pull Request Scope into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Repair the Pull Request Scope defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

A teammate acts on this belief: “A pull request should include every nearby cleanup to reduce future work.” Which repair addresses the cause?

steps.title: Sequence Pull Request Scope evidence

steps.title: Defend the Pull Request Scope model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Pull Request Scope: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
